methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series E → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.4x) to $2.8B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Space & Aerospace. Range: $1.0B - $3.5B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Space & Aerospace P/S median is 59.8x across 6 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

data.narrative:
ICEYE, a Series E space & aerospace company founded by Rafał Modrzewski and Pekka Laurila, is valued at $2.8B post-money following $650M+ in total fundraising. The valuation assessment draws on a comparable universe of 5 public-market peers (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL) and 8 private competitors across the space sector.

Two triangulation methods are applicable. Stage-progression analysis, anchoring to the $2.8B last post-money valuation and applying a typical 1.4x step-up multiplier for Series E-to-next-stage advancement, implies a forward valuation of $3.9B. Peer-median analysis across three same-sector, same/adjacent-stage private companies (Astranis at $3.5B, Apex Space and Stoke Space each at $1.0B) yields a median of $1.0B, with a range of $1.0B to $3.5B. Public-comp anchoring via P/S multiples cannot be applied, as ICEYE's revenue is not on file; the Space & Aerospace sector median P/S of 59.8x across the public comparables provides directional context only.

The triangulated valuation range spans $850M (low) to $4.5B (high), with a median of $2.5B. The current $2.8B valuation sits above the peer median, reflecting either series-stage progression or potential execution premium. Key uncertainties include ICEYE's absolute revenue scale relative to public comparables and the durability of private-market multiples in this capital-intensive sector.
